differentials.name: Cretinism
differentials.raw: Cretinism (may show epiphyseal fragmentation, much larger calcifications within epiphysis)

context: √  multiple small punctate calcifications of varying size in epiphyses (knee, hip, shoulder, wrist), base of skull, posterior elements of vertebrae, respiratory cartilage and soft tissues (neck, rib ends) before appearance of ossification centers √  prominent symmetrical shortening of femur + humerus (rarely all limbs symmetrically affected) √  congenital dislocation of hip √  multiple flexion (joint) contractures of extremities √  clubfeet √  metaphyseal splaying of proximal tubular bones (in particular about knee) √  thickening of diaphyses √  prominent vertebral + paravertebral calcificatio
